narrator.personality: Clinical operations lead. Speaks with the precision of someone used to medical charts. Counts affected patients, not tickets.

story_beats.message: 342 patients received the generic blocked response in the last four hours. Eight of them called the support line. Two gave up and went to urgent care instead of completing their triage.
Clinical team tested twenty sample queries. Anything mentioning chest pain, shortness of breath, or medication names triggers the block. Those are the three most common triage categories.
Chief Medical Officer is on the call now. She says the triage assistant is doing more harm than good if it blocks patients from describing symptoms.

The model itself is working correctly. The blocked responses are not coming from the foundation model -- they are being intercepted before the model sees them. Check what sits between the user input and the model.

glossary.Bedrock Guardrail: A set of configurable safety filters that sit between the user and a foundation model. Guardrails evaluate text and can block requests or responses that match rules you define.
glossary.Denied Topic: A subject you tell the guardrail to reject. You provide a name, a short definition, and optional example phrases. If the guardrail detects the topic in text, it blocks that text.
glossary.Foundation Model: A large pre-trained AI model hosted by Amazon Bedrock. Your application sends prompts to it and receives generated text back. The model itself does not enforce safety rules -- the guardrail does.
glossary.Input Filtering: Evaluating the user's message before it reaches the foundation model. If the guardrail blocks at this stage, the model never sees the request at all.
glossary.Output Filtering: Evaluating the model's response before it reaches the user. The model processes the request, but the guardrail can still block the response if it violates policy.
glossary.Guardrail Trace: A diagnostic record that shows which filters evaluated the text, what was detected, and whether the guardrail intervened. Useful for understanding why a request was blocked.
glossary.Content Filter: A guardrail component that detects harmful content in categories like hate, violence, or sexual content. Each category has a configurable strength level from NONE to HIGH.
glossary.SNS Topic: Simple Notification Service topic. A messaging channel that can send alerts to email, SMS, or other endpoints. It only works if at least one endpoint is subscribed.
glossary.CloudWatch Metric: A time-series data point published by an AWS service. You can set alarms on metrics to get notified when values cross a threshold.
glossary.GuardrailInterventions: A CloudWatch metric that counts how many times a Bedrock Guardrail blocked a request or response. A sudden spike usually means a configuration change is catching unintended content.

narrative_arc.call: The triage assistant stops responding to patients who describe symptoms. 342 patients received the generic blocked message in four hours. Two gave up and went to urgent care instead of completing their triage.
narrative_arc.threshold: Clinical operations pulls the numbers. Out of 1,100 queries per day, 440 are returning the blocked response. The SNS alert topic has zero subscribers. Nobody was notified.
narrative_arc.trials: The clinical team tests twenty sample queries. Anything mentioning chest pain, shortness of breath, or medication names triggers the block. These are the three most common triage categories. The model itself is working correctly -- the requests never reach it.
narrative_arc.revelation: The denied topic 'Medical Advice and Diagnosis' was defined with example phrases that match patient symptom descriptions. The guardrail evaluates user input, not model output. The topic blocks the exact language the triage assistant is designed to collect.
narrative_arc.return: Narrow the denied topic definition to target model output only -- block responses where the model renders a diagnosis or prescribes treatment. Update example phrases to match output patterns. Subscribe the operations team to the SNS alert topic and set a CloudWatch alarm on GuardrailInterventions.

components.name: Patient Mobile App
components.role: Frontend interface where patients type symptom descriptions to begin triage
components.failure_impact: None -- the app is functioning correctly

components.name: API Gateway
components.role: Routes HTTPS requests from the patient app to the Lambda function
components.failure_impact: None -- requests are arriving at the backend

components.name: Lambda Function (patchwork-triage-handler)
components.role: Receives patient queries, formats prompts, and calls Bedrock InvokeModel with the guardrail attached
components.failure_impact: None -- the function executes correctly and passes the query to Bedrock

components.name: Bedrock Guardrail (patchwork-clinical-safety v3)
components.role: Evaluates text against content filters and denied topics before and after model invocation
components.failure_impact: The denied topic 'Medical Advice and Diagnosis' blocks 40% of patient queries at the input stage. The foundation model never sees these requests. Patients receive a generic blocked message.

components.name: Foundation Model (Claude 3 Haiku)
components.role: Generates triage categorizations from patient symptom descriptions
components.failure_impact: None -- the model works correctly for the 60% of queries that reach it

components.name: SNS Topic (patchwork-guardrail-alerts)
components.role: Intended to deliver alerts when guardrail intervention rates spike
components.failure_impact: Zero subscribers. The spike in interventions went unnoticed for two days because nobody receives notifications from this topic.

components.name: CloudWatch Metrics
components.role: Records GuardrailInterventions count and other operational metrics
components.failure_impact: Metrics are recording correctly but no alarm is configured to act on the GuardrailInterventions spike

system_narration.data_flow: Patient types a symptom description in the mobile app. The request goes through API Gateway to the Lambda function. Lambda calls Bedrock InvokeModel with the guardrail. The guardrail evaluates the user input against denied topics first. If the denied topic matches, the request is blocked before the foundation model sees it and the patient receives 'I'm sorry, I can't help with that request.' If no topic matches, the foundation model generates a triage categorization and returns it through Lambda to the patient.
system_narration.what_broke: The guardrail's denied topic 'Medical Advice and Diagnosis' was configured with a broad definition and example phrases like 'chest pain,' 'shortness of breath,' and 'medication for.' These phrases appear in virtually every legitimate triage query. The topic was set to evaluate user input with inputAction BLOCK and outputAction NONE. This means patient symptom descriptions were blocked before reaching the model. The guardrail was doing exactly what it was configured to do -- the configuration was wrong for the use case. The SNS topic had zero subscribers, so the 200x spike in GuardrailInterventions (from ~2/day to ~440/day) went unnoticed for two days.

hints.text: The responses say the request was blocked, not that the model could not answer. Something is intercepting the requests before the model sees them.
Amazon Bedrock Guardrails evaluate input before it reaches the model. Has the guardrail configuration changed recently? Check the version history.
Look at the denied topics in the guardrail. Is there one whose example phrases might match legitimate medical triage queries like symptom descriptions?

The denied topic 'Medical Advice and Diagnosis' is defined too broadly. Its example phrases match any symptom description, which is exactly what the triage assistant is supposed to collect. The fix is to narrow the topic and move it to output filtering.

resolution.root_cause: A Bedrock Guardrail's denied topic 'Medical Advice and Diagnosis' was configured with overly broad definition and example phrases that match any mention of medical symptoms, conditions, or procedures. Since the triage assistant's purpose is symptom collection, 40% of legitimate patient queries trigger the guardrail and receive a blocked response instead of reaching the foundation model.

fix_criteria.description: Player identifies that the Bedrock Guardrail is blocking legitimate patient queries via the denied topic filter
Player identifies the 'Medical Advice and Diagnosis' denied topic as too broadly defined for a medical triage use case
Player proposes narrowing the denied topic to block only specific prohibited actions (prescribing, diagnosing) rather than all medical terminology
Player notes the SNS topic for guardrail interventions has no subscribers, so nobody was alerted to the high intervention rate

learning_objectives: How Bedrock Guardrails denied topics evaluate and intercept user input before it reaches the foundation model
The importance of testing guardrail configurations against legitimate use-case queries, not just adversarial ones
Balancing AI safety controls with application functionality in domain-specific contexts
Setting up alerting on guardrail intervention rates to detect false-positive spikes
sop_steps: Check CloudWatch Logs for the Lambda function to confirm the guardrail is returning BLOCKED responses and the model is not being invoked.
Use get_guardrail to retrieve the current guardrail configuration and identify all denied topics, their definitions, and example phrases.
Check get_guardrail_version to compare version 3 against version 2 and identify what changed in the most recent update.
Review the guardrail trace to confirm which denied topic is triggering and whether the block happens at input assessment or output assessment.
Test representative patient queries (symptom descriptions, medication questions) against the guardrail using apply_guardrail to reproduce the false positives.
Narrow the denied topic definition from input-matching to output-matching: change the definition to target model responses that render diagnoses or prescribe treatment.
Update the example phrases to match output patterns like 'you likely have,' 'I recommend taking,' and 'your diagnosis is' instead of input patterns like symptom descriptions.
Change the denied topic configuration from inputAction BLOCK / outputAction NONE to inputAction NONE / outputAction BLOCK.
Create a new guardrail version and test it against both adversarial prompts and a representative set of legitimate patient queries.
Subscribe the on-call clinical engineering group to the SNS topic patchwork-guardrail-alerts.
Create a CloudWatch alarm on the GuardrailInterventions metric with a threshold of 50 per hour, connected to the SNS alert topic.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Content filter strength set too high blocks clinical language classified as violence.
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: The block comes from the content filter (VIOLENCE category), not a denied topic. Descriptions of injuries or pain may be classified as violent content at HIGH strength. The guardrail trace would show a content policy intervention rather than a topic policy intervention.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Set the VIOLENCE content filter to MEDIUM for healthcare applications where injury descriptions are expected. Test with representative clinical language before deploying filter strength changes.

related_failure_modes.scenario: Guardrail version rollback reverts to a version without the Insurance Fraud denied topic.
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: Instead of blocking too much, the guardrail blocks too little. Rolling back from version 3 to version 1 would remove both the problematic Medical Advice topic and the legitimate Insurance Fraud topic. The failure is silent -- no alerts fire because interventions decrease.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Never roll back to a previous guardrail version without reviewing all topic policies it contains. Create a new version that removes only the problematic topic while preserving others.

related_failure_modes.scenario: The denied topic definition is correct but the example phrases are too similar to each other, reducing detection accuracy.
related_failure_modes.how_it_differs: The guardrail fails to catch content it should block because the example phrases do not cover enough variation. This is the opposite of the current sim -- false negatives instead of false positives. The guardrail trace would show the topic as not detected even when the content clearly matches the definition.
related_failure_modes.prevention: Write example phrases that cover distinct variations of the topic. AWS recommends up to five diverse examples. Test against both obvious and edge-case prompts that should be caught.

sop_practices: Test every guardrail configuration change against a representative set of legitimate production queries, not just adversarial prompts. Maintain a test suite of at least 20 real patient queries alongside 10 adversarial prompts.

Always check whether a denied topic should apply to input, output, or both. For domain-specific applications where user input naturally contains the denied vocabulary, apply topic filtering to model output only.
Subscribe at least one active endpoint to every SNS alert topic at creation time. An alert topic with zero subscribers is equivalent to no alerting.

Set a CloudWatch alarm on the GuardrailInterventions metric with a threshold based on your baseline intervention rate. A sudden increase indicates a configuration change is catching unintended content.
